channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Tuesday, June 2 (game #1590)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Tuesday, June 2 (game #1087)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Tuesday, June 2 (game #821)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: We went inside the Magic of Disney Animation before it opens at Disney World — and Disney is rebuilding animation as a physical experience
item.description: I went behind the walls of Disney World’s Magic of Disney Animation as it is being built and saw how Disney is reimagining the space as a multi-part attraction that lets guests step inside the animation pipeline.

item.title: Alphabet plans to raise $80 billion to pay for AI buildout
item.description: The Google parent company plans to raise the funds by selling stock.

item.title: Microsoft Build 2026 live — all the updates as they happen
item.description: We're live in San Francisco for Microsoft Build 2026.

item.title: Okay Sonos, where is my TV sound system with real left and right front speakers? Sony beat you to to the punch, the tech secretly exists in your products already — it's time to catch up
item.description: In the era of giant TVs, soundbars won't cut it for sound scale to match the pictures

item.title: Max Headroom is the godfather of AI influencers
item.description: Max Headroom was designed to glitter and stutter but today’s AI influencers are no longer visibly artificial. In 1985, three British writers, George Stone, Annabel Jankel, and Rocky Morton created Max Headroom, a glitching, stuttering synthetic personality derived from a human template for the TV show Max Headroom: 20 Minutes into the Future. They imagined him as satire—a distorted reflection of the media culture shaped by Ronald Reagan and Margaret Thatcher, where television no longer felt like just a channel, but an all-encompassing atmosphere. Wrapped in neon aesthetics and exaggerated pros...

item.title: Quote of the day by Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison: "Citizens will be on their best behavior, because we’re constantly recording and reporting everything that is going on" — a dire warning on the erosion of privacy
item.description: Legendary technologist Larry Ellison has outlined a vision for an increasingly aggressive surveillance state in the 21st century

item.title: Defense tech darling Mach Industries hits $1.8B valuation, a 4x jump in a year
item.description: In a wild ride for 22-year-old founder and CEO Ethan Thornton, Mach Industries has raised another $300 million. It already has five autonomous vehicles in development and completed a major acquisition.

item.title: Nvidia chases $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs from Microsoft, Dell, and HP
item.description: If Nvidia has cracked a way to bring AI agents easily, safely, and usefully to the masses, it could — and should — be big.

item.title: How to watch the Microsoft Build 2026 keynote with Satya Nadella live
item.description: Here's how to watch the Microsoft Build 2026 livestream on June 2, when CEO Satya Nadella will deliver the opening keynote.

item.title: 'The rise of OpenAI is attributable to a web of deceit and the exploitation of users (including Floridians)”: AG seeks to hold Sam Altman and ChatGPT accountable for harm — but it doesn't make sense to hold AI accountable for every bad outcome
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is taking a second swing at OpenAI with a new Civil Suit that claims the company released and aggressively marketed ChatGPT to the public "while concealing serious risks [and] suppressing internal safety warnings' but is AI really the villain here?
